title: Discovery Weekend

jokes.text: I'm not her brother, and I never was. Except for that one year.
jokes.setup: Mike denies being Amy's stepbrother to BuzzFeed reporter
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he technically was for one year
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike bumbling - he undermines his own defense with an unnecessary qualifier that makes it worse

jokes.text: Ben, I really don't like being told what to do. / Well, Felix Wade is the biggest bundler in the party, and he loves making millionaires square dance.
jokes.setup: Selina complains about being told what to do at square dancing
jokes.punchline: Ben explains she has to do it for money from a billionaire who enjoys humiliating other rich people
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire on political fundraising and the humiliation politicians endure for money

jokes.text: I would have invited my wife, but she's a squirter, and that dress doesn't look like it's been Scotch-guarded.
jokes.setup: Party chairman makes small talk while square dancing
jokes.punchline: Horrifically inappropriate sexual overshare about his wife
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation from mundane to disturbing - classic Veep boundary-pushing

jokes.text: For that amount of money to my campaign, I'll do-si-do for his do-si-dough.
jokes.setup: Discussion of getting money from Felix at square dancing event
jokes.punchline: Selina makes a square dance/money pun
jokes.explanation: Serviceable wordplay but fairly predictable given the context

jokes.text: Bridges made of ideas. / No wonder the rest of the world hates us.
jokes.setup: Felix's pretentious opening about building bridges of ideas
jokes.punchline: Selina's immediate cutting response to the corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about American corporate culture delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: Why? You're so skinny. / N... Oh. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina hates being called a lightweight
jokes.punchline: Gary misunderstands and compliments her physical weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - well-meaning but completely missing the point

jokes.text: Ma'am, I ran a little intel, and apparently the secret to Felix Wade is to always repeat the last two sentences that he says. / Come on, that is idiotic. He's really that insecure?
jokes.setup: Gary explains strategy to repeat Felix's last two sentences
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately does exactly what Gary said while calling it idiotic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural comedy - Selina unconsciously proves the strategy works while mocking it

jokes.text: Well, I'll tell ya a secret, it's the only reason I'm really running for president. / Really? I feel like you should have a better reason than that.
jokes.setup: Selina tries to flatter Felix by saying his event is why she's running
jokes.punchline: Felix genuinely concerned she has no better reason to be president
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment where Selina's over-the-top flattery backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: Isn't it astounding that the next President of the United States is being chosen by a closeted ex-record producer?
jokes.setup: Observing Felix Wade's influence over the election
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurdity of democracy being controlled by one rich eccentric
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about money in politics and electoral influence

jokes.text: And is it just me, or does Felix's friend seem a little young? / He'll look older after Felix switches blood with him.
jokes.punchline: Ben suggests vampiric blood exchange to explain the age difference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mild observation to absurd vampire imagery

jokes.text: Felix Wade's sexuality is the worst best-kept secret since Clyde Tolson's hysterical pregnancy. The man lives in a vast invisible closet.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor about his closet being invisible but vast
jokes.explanation: Classic Kent verbose observation with historical reference and creative metaphor

jokes.text: Wow. It always has to be about her. / Never fails. / Every time.
jokes.punchline: The team assumes Selina will somehow make Gary's illness about herself
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Selina's narcissism but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Gary vomiting noises and subsequent awkward silence
jokes.setup: Gary mentions having an upset stomach
jokes.punchline: Audible vomiting followed by uncomfortable silence
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup for Amy bulimia revelation. Functional but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: Hey, is Kim here? / No, she got fired for sleeping with you. / Buzzfeed runs a notoriously tight ship.
jokes.punchline: Mike's deadpan sarcasm about BuzzFeed having high moral standards
jokes.explanation: Good satirical jab at media outlet hypocrisy with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: What do you know about new media, Mike29748@aol.com? / Oh, no. I got hacked. It's Mike53729. / Still @aol though, right? / Until I die.
jokes.punchline: Mike's proud defiance about keeping AOL email forever
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike character moment - technologically behind but stubbornly proud of it

jokes.text: Amy's bulimic. / What?! / Well, it's about time.
jokes.punchline: Selina and Ben's callous reactions treat it like good news
jokes.explanation: Shocking character moment showing their complete lack of empathy

jokes.text: And I'll tell ya something, she might want to consider a little more purging, a little less bingeing. / God, that girl can't do anything right.
jokes.punchline: Treating bulimia as a performance issue Amy is failing at
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation treating a serious disorder as job incompetence - peak Veep cruelty

jokes.text: Amy, you got some vomit on your mustache.
jokes.punchline: Selina's blunt, unhelpful observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina tactlessness - pointing out embarrassment without offering help

jokes.text: What the Rocky Mountain fuck?!
jokes.punchline: Location-specific profanity variation
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on setting but fairly predictable profanity variation

jokes.text: Look what the Gulfstream dragged in. / Gosh, I didn't see a TED talk for fake folksiness on the schedule.
jokes.setup: Selina's sarcastic greeting referencing private jets
jokes.punchline: Tom's comeback about her artificial down-home persona
jokes.explanation: Sharp back-and-forth with good escalation and character-specific insults

jokes.text: That makes me moister than an oyster. / Plus or minus four points. / OK, still pretty moist.
jokes.setup: Selina uses crude metaphor for being pleased about poll numbers
jokes.punchline: Kent's statistical precision forces her to maintain the gross metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great interplay between Selina's crudeness and Kent's statistical obsession

jokes.text: Good Lord, what's up with Frigid Von Pole-Up-Her-Ass? / That is my Deputy Chief of Staff, Michelle York. She's basically my Amy.
jokes.punchline: Tom comparing his aide to Amy as if that's a selling point
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Amy's reputation as difficult person

jokes.text: I want all eyes on Tom now. He cannot spend another second with Felix without me jammed in between 'em like the cross-piece on an Eiffel Tower three-way. / MMF. The Devil's threesome.
jokes.setup: Selina wants to interfere with Tom and Felix's relationship
jokes.punchline: Escalates to graphic sexual metaphor that Ben enthusiastically clarifies
jokes.explanation: Selina's elaborate sexual metaphor unexpectedly matched by Ben's technical knowledge

jokes.text: She's basically my Amy. Well, my Amy has bulimia.
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately brags about Amy's eating disorder
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Selina's competitive instinct even about staff mental health issues.

jokes.text: I actually consider her to be a bit of a protégée. / Does she know that? / Thought I was your protégée. / No, you're Ben's protégée. / Not a very good one.
jokes.setup: Selina claims Senator Talbot as protégée
jokes.punchline: Reveals Amy is Ben's protégée and he rates her poorly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the casual cruelty of workplace hierarchies

jokes.text: An all-female ticket? I don't think so. The American people work hard for a living, OK? They don't need that kind of bullshit!
jokes.punchline: Selina's internalized misogyny disguised as populism
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of political hypocrisy and internalized bias

jokes.text: Did Senator Isles try and... grab your ass? / Oh, yeah. / He used to say that mine was like a candy apple.
jokes.punchline: Kemi's matter-of-fact response with disturbing specific detail
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about normalized harassment culture in politics

jokes.text: For the inappropriate... / Oh, yeah, right. 'Cause that's bad now.
jokes.setup: Learning Senator Isles is under investigation for harassment
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual acknowledgment that standards have changed
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's outdated mindset and casual amorality

jokes.text: Well, that's funny that you sprinted around the entire deck to just bump into me sight unseen.
jokes.setup: Selina pretends to accidentally encounter Tom
jokes.punchline: Tom calling out her obvious stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy inference and character interaction

jokes.text: Oh, is that so, Captain Morgan? Thank you so much for man-telling me that. / I believe the word you're fumbling for is 'mansplaining.'
jokes.punchline: Tom corrects her made-up word while mansplaining mansplaining
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about mansplaining while actually mansplaining

jokes.text: Well, science tells us that it will be difficult... but within five years, I want America... to put a man on Ellen DeGeneres.
jokes.setup: Sounds like he's going to say something about space exploration
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual pun on Ellen DeGeneres
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - tries to be clever but lands on crude and offensive

jokes.text: It's pretty good, right? / It's great, thank you. / She's a lesbian. / No, we get it. / We get the joke.
jokes.setup: Jonah proud of his crude joke
jokes.punchline: Has to explain the joke, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - explaining ruins joke and reveals his crudeness

jokes.text: Well, that could be anybody. / Sounds like you.
jokes.setup: Jonah claims the description could be anyone
jokes.punchline: Teddy immediately identifies it as Jonah
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Jonah's denial

jokes.text: Jonah, who have you traumatized? Start with the doctor who delivered you.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he's been causing trauma since birth
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalated insult implying lifelong awfulness

jokes.text: Oh, Schnozz? She was stuck up, I was just callin' her on it.
jokes.punchline: Casual use of cruel nickname while defending harassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah obliviousness - uses cruel nickname while denying wrongdoing

jokes.text: Oh, Monster Tits and Monster Tits, Jr.? But all I did there was express some genuine concern that they had bras big enough to handle all that meat. / At the Congressional Prayer Breakfast. / Yeah. It was exactly what Jesus would have done.
jokes.punchline: Claims Jesus would have made the same comments at prayer breakfast
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah - escalates from crude to blasphemous while maintaining innocence

jokes.text: if anyone asks, tell 'em you've been chemically castrated. It's very easy to lie about. Believe me, nobody checks.
jokes.setup: Advising Jonah on handling harassment allegations
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he lie about castration and admitting personal experience
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny advice with unexpected personal revelation from Teddy

jokes.text: In current gay parlance, Dan presents somewhere between a 'wolf' and an 'otter,' some would call a 'frost otter.'
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Dan is Felix's type
jokes.punchline: Kent's clinical analysis of gay subculture terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Kent moment - academic analysis of sexual subcultures

jokes.text: Nice try, Grandma, but you aged out of Felix's demo back in the 20th century. / Uh, I'm 39-and-a-half. / You may want to start coloring that gray.
jokes.setup: Felix dismisses Dan as too old
jokes.punchline: Dan's defensive response about his age gets more criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's vanity being attacked

jokes.text: Yeah. It was exactly what Jesus would have done. I mean, he's a man too, Teddy.
jokes.setup: Jonah's behavior at Congressional Prayer Breakfast
jokes.punchline: Claims Jesus would also comment on women's breasts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly blasphemous Jonah logic - using religious justification for sexual harassment.

jokes.text: And I would give you one word of advice, which is to try laxatives. / I'll tell you why. It saves your teeth.
jokes.setup: Selina offers advice about Amy's bulimia
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely practical advice about dental damage from vomiting
jokes.explanation: Shockingly practical advice that shows Selina's complete lack of empathy

jokes.text: I'm pregnant. / Can you imagine? I'm pregnant. / That's not funny. / Now I'm gonna be sick.
jokes.punchline: Gary's reaction causes Selina to feel nauseous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with appropriate character reactions

jokes.text: Dan fucked you? / Mm-hmm. / What, were you wearing a full-length mirror?
jokes.punchline: Implies Dan is so narcissistic he'd need to see himself during sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific insult that perfectly captures Dan's narcissism

jokes.text: Listen, I did not spend my entire life defending a woman's right to choose for you to choose this.
jokes.punchline: Selina's twisted take on pro-choice politics
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly twisted political logic that perfectly captures Selina's self-centered worldview

jokes.text: Do you know that there's one girl that worked in my office who had four kids. / What was she, a possum?
jokes.setup: Felix mentions employee with four children
jokes.punchline: Selina compares her to an animal known for large litters
jokes.explanation: Sharp but predictable animal comparison for multiple births

jokes.text: The thing I realized about hip hop before anyone else is that the artists would sign away the publishing rights for next to nothing! / That's an incredible anecdote!
jokes.setup: Felix tells boring story about exploiting artists
jokes.punchline: Selina's sarcastic enthusiasm while repeating his sentences strategy
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the sentence repetition strategy with appropriate sarcasm

jokes.text: But it takes a brave person to be able to see one's shortcomings. / Yeah, it does. / In this day and age, it's very, very refre... / Remarkable. / Refreshing. / Refreshing was the word I meant.
jokes.punchline: Selina anticipating and correcting his word choice while following the repetition strategy
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the callback where Selina gets too into the strategy

jokes.text: You are a stone-cold, neck-down hottie. I want the whole world to know we dated.
jokes.setup: Jonah refusing to sign NDA about not dating Amanda
jokes.punchline: His gross compliment and delusional pride about their non-relationship
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah delusion and inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: I split the bill on all my dates. Why would I pay for a girl to get fatter?
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely practical reasoning about not paying for women's food
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Jonah logic that reveals his complete awfulness

jokes.text: God, Jamaican jerk Jesus, just sign this! / No, Teddy! Jonah Ryan will not be silenced.
jokes.punchline: Jonah's grandiose declaration about not being silenced for fake dating
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jonah's inflated sense of importance

jokes.text: Did you offer to blow him? / Tut-tut, Selina. We do not refer to Mr. Wade's sexual orientation. / Sorry. / But yes. Yes, I did.
jokes.setup: Selina asks if Tom offered sexual favors to Felix
jokes.punchline: Tom scolds her for the reference then admits he did it
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection where Tom's propriety is immediately undercut by his admission

jokes.text: Well, I didn't tell him you were a lousy lay. / Probably read it in our books.
jokes.setup: Selina's retort about not badmouthing Tom sexually
jokes.punchline: Tom's reference to their tell-all books revealing their past relationship
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback referencing their public memoirs

jokes.text: Selina, I mean it. You're in my head all the time. You always have been and you always will be. / Yeah, yeah. / I love you. / Ma'am.
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive response and Gary's awkward interruption
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with awkward timing

jokes.text: She's having a textbook aneurysm. / Still looks like a goddess.
jokes.punchline: Gary's devotion remains despite medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - blind devotion even during crisis

jokes.text: That was the most humiliating experience I've had in my entire life, and I was Vice President of the United States...
jokes.punchline: Comparing it unfavorably to being Vice President
jokes.explanation: Great callback to show's premise with perfect character logic

jokes.text: Which, I was all for it when it killed my dad, but it's not so funny when the shoe's on the other ventricle.
jokes.punchline: Admits he was happy when heart disease killed his father, plus anatomy pun
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark admission combined with clever anatomical wordplay

jokes.text: Which, I was all for it when it killed my dad, but it's not so funny when the shoe's on the other ventricle.
jokes.punchline: Admits approval of father's death, anatomically-correct idiom variation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark confession with clever medical wordplay on common idiom.

jokes.text: When I had my face-lift, we told people that you were having a face-lift.
jokes.setup: Discussion of their various medical cover-ups
jokes.punchline: Using Selina as a decoy for his own vanity surgery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing their mutual manipulation. Nice reveal about their relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: When I had my face-lift, we told people that you were having a face-lift.
jokes.setup: Discussion of covering up medical procedures
jokes.punchline: Tom used Selina as his cover story
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurdity of political cover-ups

jokes.text: And then we blow each other's brain's out. / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Tom's fantasy of suburban life with Selina
jokes.punchline: Mutual suicide as the logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark escalation of romantic fantasy to violent conclusion

jokes.text: Is it gonna explode between my legs and make me cum until I cry? / I think it's peppermint.
jokes.setup: Selina asks about bath bomb in crude terms
jokes.punchline: Gary's innocent, practical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Selina's crudeness and Gary's innocence

jokes.text: I mean, you pull it out to cum on her tits 'cause you think it's gonna make you feel alive. But it doesn't matter where you cum, Ames. Ya know? It just doesn't. It just... It doesn't.
jokes.setup: Dan having existential crisis about meaningless sex
jokes.punchline: Graphic sexual details delivered as philosophical observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward moment of Dan's crude self-reflection

jokes.text: They could reassure people that look like him that their country wouldn't be ruined by people that look like her.
jokes.setup: Tom and Kemi as potential running mates
jokes.punchline: Selina's cynical racial analysis of electoral appeal
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary on racial dynamics in electoral politics delivered with Selina's cynicism.

jokes.text: How about we disrupt the election? / I'm addicted to disruption, especially since I stopped using cocaine.
jokes.punchline: Felix's casual admission of cocaine addiction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with unexpected personal revelation

jokes.text: A lot of you knew me when I was really little and I was just running around in diapers. And now here we are, and a lot of you are wearin' diapers.
jokes.punchline: Insulting his elderly base by referencing their incontinence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - accidentally insulting his own supporters

jokes.text: 'Lyin' Ryan.' That's gonna stick.
jokes.punchline: Recognition that the nickname will become permanent
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about political nicknames

jokes.text: But finally, people are starting to believe women, and I believe Amanda when she says, 'Not me. Not. Me.' / That's catchy too.
jokes.punchline: Teddy appreciating it as a campaign slogan
jokes.explanation: Good satirical moment about serious issues becoming marketing

jokes.text: The first one since Carter-Mondale.
jokes.punchline: Felix's confused historical reference (Carter-Mondale were both men)
jokes.explanation: Subtle joke about Felix not knowing basic political history

jokes.text: I'm just about to fuck this really hot 19-year-old cater waitress. OK? Oh, and get this. Her name's Meagan.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to discuss their relationship
jokes.punchline: Dan's crude dismissal including the waitress's generic name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment showing his complete selfishness

jokes.text: No one. Literally no one ever wants prosecco.
jokes.punchline: Ben's emphatic rejection of the drink
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor delivered with perfect deadpan

jokes.text: you're gonna be drowning in money so dark it could get shot entering its own apartment.
jokes.punchline: Racial profiling reference about the money being 'dark'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor combining political finance terms with racial commentary

jokes.text: Seriously, it's America's first two-cooter ticket.
jokes.punchline: Crude anatomical terminology for historic political moment
jokes.explanation: Typical Ben crudeness reducing historic moment to anatomy

jokes.text: How dare that smooth shitsack cheat on his wife and risk his political future with someone that's not me?
jokes.punchline: Selina's outrage is about not being chosen, not the moral issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina narcissism - making someone else's affair about her rejection

jokes.text: Just because Felix Wade is gayer than an Eames chair in assless leather chaps doesn't mean that he's some sort of fashion diva.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Felix's gayness and fashion sense
jokes.punchline: Elaborate gay stereotype with design furniture reference
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of gay stereotypes with unexpected design reference

jokes.text: Thousands of unattractive women.
jokes.setup: Reporter mentions thousands of women joining #NotMe
jokes.punchline: Jonah's immediate dismissal of them as unattractive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah response - missing the point while being offensive

jokes.text: I stand behind these brave, unattractive women who have come forward to say, 'Not me.' / Once upon a time, back in high school, I too did not date Jonah Ryan.
jokes.punchline: Admits she also didn't date her own son in high school
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward moment of mother accidentally roasting her son

jokes.text: Looks like we're not the only campaign who farted and had to throw their pants away.
jokes.punchline: Richard's elaborate metaphor for political embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Classic Richard over-elaborate metaphor for simple concept

jokes.text: Where is that fat-faced, freckled fuck-it-up-agus?
jokes.punchline: Elaborate alliterative insult ending in made-up suffix
jokes.explanation: Creative Selina insult with alliteration and made-up word ending

jokes.text: Hiding in the bathroom, ma'am, making pretend diarrhea noises.
jokes.punchline: Mike's elaborate avoidance strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike cowardice with unnecessarily specific detail

jokes.text: I just can't believe that that second-hand Muppet wrote an article that people actually read.
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive description of Mike as discount puppet
jokes.explanation: Creative insult comparing Mike to used children's toy

jokes.text: I'm gonna choke him with that giant cock of his, see how he likes it.
jokes.punchline: Graphic threat using gay sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Crude threat that shows Selina's anger and lack of filter

jokes.text: Because back in our old Senate days, this one here had a real thing for blond hill rats in Nine West pumps desperate to fuck their way to the middle.
jokes.punchline: Specific description of Tom's past romantic preferences
jokes.explanation: Brutal character assassination with specific class and brand references

jokes.text: Congratulations. Then you probably won't fuck it up.
jokes.setup: Learning Michelle is Deputy Chief of Staff
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about competence
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina passive-aggressive compliment

jokes.text: God, I feel so sorry for whoever's stickin' their dick in that bag of mayonnaise.
jokes.punchline: Grotesque description of Michelle's sexual appeal
jokes.explanation: Disgusting but memorable Selina insult with food imagery

jokes.text: The tri-racial twat stabbed me in the back! / She really is your protégée.
jokes.punchline: Tom suggesting this behavior shows she learned from Selina
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback showing how Selina's influence corrupts others

jokes.text: Oh, remember, lift with your back. / It's your legs. L-Legs. / No, you lift with your back. / Oh. That's incorrect. / OK, well. That could probably be the main cause of your scoliosis. / I have scoliosis? / Oh, yes. Clearly.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to diagnosing Gary with spinal condition
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from bad advice to casual medical diagnosis

jokes.text: That could probably be the main cause of your scoliosis. I have scoliosis? Oh, yes. Clearly.
jokes.punchline: Selina diagnoses Richard with spinal condition he didn't know he had
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat showing Selina's casual cruelty in medical 'diagnosis' of staff.

tmdb_overview: At an Aspen retreat for rich donors, Selina deals with potential adversaries and allies. Amy's behavior raises Gary's suspicions.
insight: Veep sustains 87 jokes across 38 minutes, hitting 82.4 with escalating character chaos as the only engine.
